Ceanothus americanus, also known as New Jersey tea, is a shrub native to eastern North America, valued for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Ceanothus americanus include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Ceanothus americanus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The most common herbal preparations made with Ceanothus americanus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a variety of health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
